scope: HIP/CK source-boundary compressed sparse-MLA backward dKV path on 1xMI350, layer 2, S4096, H64, D512, topk512, compressed_groups1024
read: The valid dKV mode proves the source-boundary HIP/CK score page can be consumed inside the same workgroup with correct dKV on S128, but the S4096 latency is 25.94 ms and fails the 11.13 ms Triton full-boundary gate. The 256-thread split shows global dKV writes add about 4.17 ms, while the contribution-consumer side adds about 14.69 ms over the mode-2 score/prob/dP producer. The newer 512-thread mode6 valid-dKV result is 24.26 ms versus 23.65 ms for matching no-write mode5, so final global dKV write/atomic overhead is only about 0.61 ms after this same-CTA widening. A 1024-thread four-D16 no-write variant regresses further to 31.36 ms. The problem is therefore not just atomics; ordinary one-CTA score-page reuse either serializes D consumption or becomes too heavy when widened.
decision: Do not promote this ordinary same-workgroup mode. Return to the Triton baseline's query-major decomposition as the schedule contract, then implement it in CK/rocWMMA/HIP specifically to control the dKV epilogue: token/head/selected-window tiles keep q and grad_output hot, the score/prob/scaled-dP page is consumed before HBM, and selected-slot contributions are reduced by compressed group before global writes. TileLang and late dKV-only reducers remain secondary until this source-boundary producer-consumer layout beats the 11.13 ms boundary gate.
